journal_entry.description: Board declared cash dividend payable in 30 days
journal_entry.rationale: When a cash dividend is declared, the company incurs a legal obligation to pay shareholders. Under IFRS, the declaration creates a liability (Dividends Payable) and reduces retained earnings through the Dividends account. The debit tuple shows b=1 (dividend increase), meaning we debit the Dividends account. The credit tuple shows a=1 (liability increase), meaning we credit Dividends Payable. No cash changes hands yet—payment occurs in 30 days. This is not a taxable transaction, so no tax lines are required.

output.reason: When a dividend is declared, the debit side records a dividend increase (not yet paid). The transaction creates: DEBIT Dividends (or Retained Earnings under some treatments) and CREDIT Dividends Payable. Since dividends are increased by debits, this is 1 dividend increase line.

debit_classifier.reason: When a dividend is declared, the debit side records a dividend increase (not yet paid). The transaction creates: DEBIT Dividends (or Retained Earnings under some treatments) and CREDIT Dividends Payable. Since dividends are increased by debits, this is 1 dividend increase line.

output.reason: When a cash dividend is declared but not yet paid, a liability (Dividends Payable) is created. Since the dividend will be paid in 30 days, we credit Dividends Payable = liability increase. The debit side (not classified here) would increase Dividends/Retained Earnings account.

credit_classifier.reason: When a cash dividend is declared but not yet paid, a liability (Dividends Payable) is created. Since the dividend will be paid in 30 days, we credit Dividends Payable = liability increase. The debit side (not classified here) would increase Dividends/Retained Earnings account.

output.description: Board declared cash dividend payable in 30 days
output.rationale: When a cash dividend is declared, the company incurs a legal obligation to pay shareholders. Under IFRS, the declaration creates a liability (Dividends Payable) and reduces retained earnings through the Dividends account. The debit tuple shows b=1 (dividend increase), meaning we debit the Dividends account. The credit tuple shows a=1 (liability increase), meaning we credit Dividends Payable. No cash changes hands yet—payment occurs in 30 days. This is not a taxable transaction, so no tax lines are required.

output.reason: Entry correctly records dividend declaration. Debit to Dividends for $500,000 (decreases equity through retained earnings), credit to Dividends Payable for $500,000 (creates liability). Entry balances (debits = credits). Accounts are appropriate - dividend declarations create a legal obligation (liability) at declaration date, not at payment date. Amount matches transaction text exactly. No tax lines needed as dividend declarations are not taxable transactions. Directionality correct for all account types.
